reasoning:
Based on the FIDE rankings Wikipedia page as of June 8, 2025, Gukesh's rating is listed as 2776 (reflecting a drop due to a poor performance in the May Superbet tournament). A 1% increase requires his rating to reach 2803.76, meaning he needs an official FIDE rating of at least 2804. Achieving 2804 is an extremely high bar, historically reached by very few players. 

For June 15: The Wikipedia page will predictably not update its numerical ratings until the next FIDE official release on July 1. His rating will remain 2776 on Wikipedia on June 15. The probability of it resolving YES is functionally zero (adjusted to the 0.05 minimum). 

For July 8: Gukesh performed well in the recently concluded Norway Chess (late May/early June), likely scoring +3 in classical games against top opposition. This strong performance should recover his lost points and raise his live rating by around 15-18 points to back near the ~2793 mark. When July 1 ratings are published, he will be considerably short of the 2804 target, making a YES resolution on July 8 extremely improbable (0.06). 

For September 6 and December 5: Gukesh will play more elite events (e.g., Grand Chess Tour stops). Given he has peaked at 2794 previously, achieving 2804 is possible if he goes on a dominant winning streak, which he is certainly capable of as World Champion. The probability gradually increases to reflect growing uncertainty over a longer horizon.
